name: Wales (Saint David's Cross, alternative)

colors.symbolism: the gold cross of Saint David

description: A black field with a gold (yellow) cross extending to the edges.
history: Used as the flag of the Diocese of St Davids in Wales since the medieval period; revived as a national symbol of Wales (in addition to the Red Dragon flag) in the late 20th century. Often flown on Saint David's Day (1 March).

features.symbolism: the gold cross on a black field, attributed to the patron saint of Wales
